finding_summary.description: Full page of answer explanations (解答3-11〜3-15) present in OCR but silently dropped by structuring stage — structured/translated/output all empty. Release-impacting.
JP choice-letter prefixes (ウ/エ) bleed into ZH choice fields in blocks 1 and 2; propagates into output release. Should be A/B/C/D.

EN choice labels for Q0/Q1 show duplicate lowercase 'a./b./c./d.' artifact ('A. a. DRAM...'). ZH choices blocks 1-2 retain JP ウ/エ prefixes. Propagates to output. Answer-key line at page bottom partially blank in OCR (low severity, answer_index correct in JSON).
'コレが出る！' callout badge captured in OCR but absent from all translated entity fields. ZH choices Q1 retain JP-label prefixes. Not release-impacting (callout is metadata, not exam content).
ZH choice labels mix 甲/乙/丙/丁 and A/B/C/D inconsistently across blocks — cosmetic, not semantic.
Same ZH label inconsistency (甲/乙 vs A/B) across blocks — cosmetic only.
OCR header shows book print-page 524; pipeline ID is 540. Known sequential offset, not an error.
Single question page, clean OCR, correct answer_index=1 (SDGs).

cross_cutting_patterns: JP choice-letter prefix leakage into ZH fields (ウ/エ→C/D) affects pages 204, 341, 416 — likely a systemic translation-prompt issue.
ZH label inconsistency (甲/乙/丙/丁 vs A/B/C/D) affects pages 43, 86 — different question blocks on same page use different conventions.
Page 136 content dropout is an isolated structuring-stage failure, not a pattern seen on other pages in this batch.
recommended_actions: CRITICAL: Re-run structuring+translation for page 136 — 5 answer-explanation blocks missing from release.
HIGH: Fix JP-label prefix stripping in translation prompt/post-processing for ZH choice fields (affects pages 204, 341, 416).
HIGH: Fix EN choice-label duplication artifact 'A. a.' in page 416.
LOW: Standardize ZH choice label convention to A/B/C/D across all pages.
